title: Tiger's Return Falls Short as LA Golf Club Clinches 2026 SoFi Cup
excerpt: Tiger Woods made his long-awaited TGL comeback but LA Golf Club proved unstoppable, sweeping Jupiter Links 9-2 across two Finals matches to claim the SoFi Cup.
body:
LA Golf Club Seal Historic TGL Title

Los Angeles Golf Club claimed the 2026 TGL SoFi Cup with ruthless efficiency on Wednesday night, sweeping Jupiter Links 2-0 in the Finals and capping a dominant sophomore season for the tech-meets-golf league. The final match was a 9-2 demolition decided in just ten of a possible fifteen holes — but the evening’s defining narrative belonged to the man on the losing side.

Tiger’s Return: Bright Start, Bitter End

Tiger Woods, sidelined since tearing his Achilles in spring 2025 and subsequently undergoing lumbar disc replacement surgery last October, inserted himself into the Jupiter Links lineup for Match 2, replacing Kevin Kisner. The early signs were encouraging. A flush 3-wood from 279 yards on the opener, a lagged 45-footer conceded as a birdie at the second — Jupiter led 2-0 and Woods was very much a factor.

Then came the Cenote. Hole seven. A 3.5-foot putt that should have extended the lead slid agonisingly past the cup. Woods slammed his putter into the synthetic turf, and LAGC sensed blood immediately. They took a 3-2 lead and never surrendered it. What followed was a masterclass: three consecutive eagles over holes eight, nine and ten. Sahith Theegala drove the green at the par-4 Bluebonnet; Justin Rose put a 5-wood from 255 yards to inside five feet at the tenth and the title was won.

Match 2 Final Score
9–2
LAGC Team Prize
$9M
Consecutive Eagles
3
Holes Played
10 of 15

The Winning Side

For Rose, Theegala and Tommy Fleetwood, the SoFi Cup triumph meant $2.25 million apiece from a $9 million team prize. Rose’s composed clincher belied the drama that preceded it; Theegala was irrepressible, his eagle at the par-4 eighth the moment that broke Jupiter’s resolve. Fleetwood, who admitted after Match 1 that LAGC had “got away with one”, had no such reservations after the sweep.

Tiger Speaks

“We got our ass kicked at the end. Three eagles in a row and we didn’t respond. I missed a short one at the beginning to give them momentum and we never got it back.”

Woods was characteristically blunt post-match, adding that his body “doesn’t recover like it did when it was 24, 25.” The looming question — one that will dominate golf conversation from now until Augusta week — is whether he will tee it up at the Masters on April 9. He offered no indication either way.

Eyes on Augusta

The Texas Children’s Houston Open is under way at Memorial Park, with Scottie Scheffler absent ahead of the birth of his second child. The Masters beckons on April 9 and Woods’ TGL return confirmed he can swing at full tilt — but Augusta’s demanding terrain is a different proposition entirely. Golf’s greatest question mark endures.

Apr 9–12
The Masters Tournament — Augusta National Golf Club, Augusta, Georgia
